learning_outcomes.description: Critically evaluate curriculum, assessment and pedagogic practice in light of how students learn in specific higher education contexts
Design engaging and inclusive learning activities and assessment
Evaluate and enhance personal teaching practice
Collaborate with members of a community of practice in teaching and learning at UQ.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will prepare a briefing document that contextualises and informs the curriculum re-/design you are undertaking in this course. Your document should position the curriculum within its broader context, justify the need for re-/design, and consider key stakeholders involved in the process.

Your briefing document should draw on program preparatory work, initial readings, and discussions from class. 

AI permitted use: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit through the Turnitin link in your Ultra course site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. If you anticipate any difficulties meeting the due date, please contact the Course Coordinator as early as possible to discuss your options. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In this task, you will review an assessment task (excluding exams) to evaluate its alignment with learning outcomes, identify areas for improvement, and propose evidence-based enhancements. Your review should reflect PSF2023 values and knowledge and draw on pedagogical theory, educational research, and institutional policy.

If re-designing an existing course, choose an assessment from that course. If designing a new course, review an assessment from another course or consult your Course Coordinator for guidance.

AI permitted use: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit through the Turnitin link in your Blackboard Ultra course site. Refer to the grading rubric on the course BlackBoard Ultra site for assessment criteria.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. If you anticipate any difficulties meeting the due date, please contact the Course Coordinator as early as possible to discuss your options. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
15/07/2026 1:00 pm

The Showcase Presentation is during scheduled during class time, and the reflection will be completed in class using Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
The Showcase and Reflection assessment provides you with a collaborative opportunity to articulate, evaluate, and refine your curriculum (re)design through peer discussion, feedback, and critical reflection. This assessment has two parts:

Part 1: Showcase Presentation (20minutes). Share and justify your curriculum (re)design with peers, receive formative feedback, and engage in reflective discussion to inform your final presentation.

Part 2: Showcase Reflection (approximately 500 words). Reflect on your Showcase experience, summarise peer feedback, identify improvements to your design, and consider what you’ve learned from others’ presentations.

AI permitted use: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Showcase Presentation will be held on 15 July during the scheduled class time, with the Showcase Reflection completed in class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. If you anticipate any difficulties meeting the due date, please contact the Course Coordinator as early as possible to discuss your options. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
As the final activity of the Curriculum Design at UQ course, you will create a pre-recorded presentation (10 minutes) discussing your curriculum re-/design.

Your audience includes peers, learning designers, and Teaching & Learning committee members, to whom you will justify your curriculum re-/design.

AI permitted use: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit through the link in your Blackboard Ultra course site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. If you anticipate any difficulties meeting the due date, please contact the Course Coordinator as early as possible to discuss your options. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 1: Curriculum design for learning

In this workshop, we will cover course aims, assessment and activities organisation, the principles and practice of backwards design, the importance of context, and compelling questions for curriculum development. We will also focus on learners and their learning and how you can set the scene for your learners.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 1: Curriculum design for learning In this workshop, we will cover course aims, assessment and activities organisation, the principles and practice of backwards design, the importance of context, and compelling questions for curriculum development. We will also focus on learners and their learning and how you can set the scene for your learners.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 2: Designing Learning

In this workshop, we will explore developing and aligning quality learning outcomes and assessment principles. You will also have time to work on Assessment 1


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 2: Designing Learning In this workshop,  we will explore developing and aligning quality learning outcomes and assessment principles. You will also have time to work on Assessment 1

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 3: Assessing learning

In this workshop we will explore assessment in practice, drawing on principles of constructive alignment. We focus on assessment design, grading, rubrics, and feedback as well as the limits and opportunities of AI with regards to assessment.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 3: Assessing learning In this workshop we will explore assessment in practice, drawing on principles of constructive alignment. We focus on assessment design, grading, rubrics, and feedback as well as the limits and opportunities of AI with regards to assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 4: Pedagogy

In this workshop, you will discuss and apply pedagogical principles and apply pedagogical principles in practice.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 4: Pedagogy In this workshop, you will discuss and apply pedagogical principles and apply pedagogical principles in practice.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 5: Bringing it all together

In this workshop, you will bring together all the learning from the course and prepare your design with support

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 5: Bringing it all together In this workshop, you will bring together all the learning from the course and prepare your design with support

learning_activities.topic:
Showcase Presentation and Reflection

In this Showcase, you will present your curriculum (re-)design and engage in peer feedback conversations. Attendance is mandatory.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
